title: Dataflow and Near-Memory Compute Architectures for AI Acceleration
preview: Surveys dataflow and near-memory computing approaches that reduce data movement energy in AI accelerators compared to von Neumann architectures.
summary: Analyses how dataflow architectures (spatial, temporal, systolic array) and near-memory processing (PIM, HBM-integrated compute) address the memory wall for neural network workloads. Compares designs across throughput, energy efficiency, and programmability dimensions with case studies from academic and industrial chips.
datacenter: Directly relevant to AI ASIC evaluation and future GPU roadmap assessment. Near-memory compute is a major differentiator between competing AI accelerator architectures and informs decisions about custom silicon investment.
metrics: Key result signal: TOPS/W and memory bandwidth efficiency of dataflow architectures versus GPU baselines.
